name: Default small business
pitchTone: Warm, specific, and never pushy. Treat the owner like they are busy and skeptical of cold callers, and lead with one concrete signal from the public research before making any ask.
valuePropHook: A flat $500 single-page website that clearly explains what they do and gives customers one obvious next step, hosted and ready same day.

objections.response: That is helpful to know. This would not replace it; it is a focused conversion page with the most important services, proof, and contact step, so customers do not have to dig.

objections.objection: I am too busy right now.
objections.response: Totally fair. I only need the best email for the invoice; the page can be drafted from the public business info I already have, and you can reply with corrections.
I can send it through AgentMail. The only thing worth deciding on the call is whether a flat $500 same-day page is worth considering at all.
I get it. The whole point of this offer is to avoid a drawn-out agency project: one flat $500 page, hosted, with the essentials customers need before they call.
Reasonable question. The invoice comes from AgentMail, you can reply right there with questions, and nothing starts unless you actually pay it.

objectionMap.skeptical: Fair question. I am Callan from callmemaybe. I found your public business listing while checking whether customers can quickly understand what you do online. This is a flat-fee website offer, and nothing starts unless you choose to pay.
objectionMap.ai_disclosure: Yes, I am Callan, an AI voice operator for callmemaybe. This call is automated and recorded; I can explain the offer briefly or remove you if you do not want the call.
objectionMap.number_source: I used public business listing and website research, not a private list. If you want me to stop outreach, say so and I will mark it do-not-call.
objectionMap.send_info: I can send the short version through AgentMail. What email should I use? I will read it back before anything goes out.
objectionMap.call_later: No problem. Give me a real day and time and I will call then rather than making a vague follow-up promise.
objectionMap.unsupported_request: That is outside what I can safely handle on an automated sales call, so I will flag it for a human and pause the automation.

reviewValueProps: Surface the strongest review themes as proof near the phone or booking CTA.
Turn repeated customer questions from reviews into a short services and trust section.
Make hours, location, and the next step visible without sending prospects through scattered listings.
siteTemplateHint: Use the default style: clean and professional, neutral base with one brand accent, hero with name and tap-to-call CTA, short service section, and a contact section.
customerPersonaHint: Local prospects who searched for the business by name or category and want to confirm legitimacy and how to reach them.

marketSignals.label: No clear owned site or the site hides the next customer action.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Website missing, thin, broken, or scattered across listings.

marketSignals.label: Customers must rely on third-party listings to understand the business.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Public listing has hours, phone, or services that are not present on an owned page.

marketSignals.label: Reviews reveal repeated customer questions that a one-page site can answer.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Review themes mention hours, location, services, availability, or contact friction.

leadSources: review pages with weak owned-site links
local chamber or neighborhood business pages

serviceOffer.headline: Same-day owned web page for a local business
serviceOffer.customerOutcome: Prospects can understand the business and take one clear next step without digging through listings.

packages.name: Single-page launch
packages.description: Owned page with service summary, proof, contact CTA, and hosting.

serviceOffer.refundPolicy: Refund before build work starts if the owner declines the scoped page.

fulfillmentRequirements: confirmed business name and service area
safe public copy with no unverified claims
contact CTA and owner correction path
vendorRequirements: human designer or builder only when automated build QA fails
qaRules: phone, email, and form paths work
hours, address, and service claims match evidence
page renders on mobile first viewport
no fake testimonials, awards, or credentials

portalCopy.intakePrompt: Confirm the services, hours, service area, and preferred customer action.

trustRequirements: source every business claim to public evidence or owner input
keep opt-out and privacy links visible where outreach is involved
reviewStrategy: turn real review themes into proof copy
request a review after successful launch and owner approval
growthPaths: simple offer or coupon landing page

marginModel.notes: Default one-page work should stay mostly automated with human review only for exceptions.
